Chronicles of the
Endangered
A Premium Wildlife Collection

Chronicles of the Endangered

Copyright © 2026
All rights reserved.
ISBN 978-1-234567-89-0

No part of this publication may be reproduced, distributed, or transmitted in any form or by any means without the prior written permission of the publisher.

This book is intended for educational purposes. All wildlife information has been sourced from IUCN Red List, WWF, and other conservation organizations. All wildlife photographs sourced from Wikimedia Commons under Creative Commons licenses (CC BY 4.0, CC BY-SA 4.0, CC0) and are used in compliance with their respective license terms. Individual photo attributions are available upon request. Population estimates and conservation statuses are based on the most recent available data and may change. Every effort has been made to ensure accuracy.

African Forest Elephant

Critically Endangered
Est. Population approx. 145,000
Primary Threat Poaching & Habitat Loss
Where Found Central Africa (Gabon, ROC)

Amur Leopard

Critically Endangered
Est. Population approx. 100
Primary Threat Poaching & Habitat Loss
Where Found Russian Far East & China

Black Rhinoceros

Critically Endangered
Est. Population approx. 5,600
Primary Threat Poaching for horns
Where Found Sub-Saharan Africa

Giant Anteater

Vulnerable
Est. Population approx. 5,000
Primary Threat Habitat Loss & Road Kills
Where Found Central & South America

Sumatran Tiger

Critically Endangered
Est. Population fewer than 400
Primary Threat Poaching & Habitat Loss
Where Found Sumatra, Indonesia

Cross River Gorilla

Critically Endangered
Est. Population 200 - 300
Primary Threat Hunting & Habitat Loss
Where Found Nigeria & Cameroon border

Sumatran Orangutan

Critically Endangered
Est. Population approx. 14,600
Primary Threat Deforestation
Where Found Sumatra, Indonesia

Tapanuli Orangutan

Critically Endangered
Est. Population fewer than 800
Primary Threat Mining & Hydroelectric projects
Where Found Batang Toru Forest, Sumatra

Critically Endangered
Est. Population fewer than 100
Primary Threat Oil exploration & Hunting
Where Found Niger

Critically Endangered
Est. Population approx. 200

"
The greatness of a nation and its moral progress can be judged by the way its animals are treated.
Mahatma Gandhi
🌿
"The greatness of a nation and its moral progress can be judged by the way its animals are treated."
Mahatma Gandhi
A stunning visual tribute to the world's most endangered species. Featuring 69 remarkable animals from every corner of the globe, this premium collection raises awareness about the urgent need for wildlife conservation.
ISBN 978-1-234567-89-0
